Ticket Description: Teachers should be able to access the Non-Practising Membership application form through the Dashboard accreditation menu. This is the main entry point for the application.
Step Action: Navigate to the Dashboard
Step Expected Result: The Dashboard loads successfully | The 'Your dashboards' heading is visible | The accreditation level menu (Proficient Teacher, Highly Accomplished, or Lead Teacher) appears in the left navigation

Step Action: Click the accreditation level in the left navigation menu
Step Expected Result: The accreditation submenu expands | The 'Create New Application' link appears

Step Action: Click 'Create New Application'
Step Expected Result: The application selection page appears | The 'Non-practising teacher' option is visible with a description

Step Action: Click the 'Non-practising teacher' option
Step Expected Result: The Non-Practising Membership application form opens | The page title 'Application for Non Practising Accreditation' is displayed

TestCase Ticket Summary: Verify Application Form Layout and Content
Ticket Description: The Non-Practising Membership application form should display all required fields, information, and buttons needed to complete the application.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership application form
Step Expected Result: The form title 'Application for Non Practising Accreditation' is visible | The information section explains the Non-practising status and its effects on the NSW Public Register of Teachers | The information section includes a link to the NESA website | The 'Start Date' field is present | The 'Reason for Non-Practising Status' dropdown is present | The declaration section is visible and unchecked | The 'Save Draft' button is present and disabled | The 'Submit Application' button is present and disabled | The 'Back' button is present

TestCase Ticket Summary: Past Start Date Prevents Application Submission
Ticket Description: Teachers should not be able to use a past date as the start date for their Non-Practising Membership, even if other fields are completed.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership application form
Step Expected Result: The 'Save Draft' button is disabled | The 'Submit Application' button is disabled

Step Action: Enter a past date (for example: 01/01/2020) in the 'Start Date' field
Step Expected Result: The past date is entered in the 'Start Date' field

Step Action: Select any reason from the 'Reason for Non-Practising Status' dropdown
Step Expected Result: A reason is selected | The 'Save Draft' button remains disabled | The 'Submit Application' button remains disabled

Step Action: Check the declaration checkbox
Step Expected Result: The declaration is now checked | The 'Save Draft' button remains disabled | The 'Submit Application' button remains disabled

TestCase Ticket Summary: Start Date Only — Application Cannot be Submitted
Ticket Description: When only the start date is entered without a reason or declaration, the application should remain blocked from saving or submitting.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership application form
Step Expected Result: The 'Save Draft' button is disabled | The 'Submit Application' button is disabled

Step Action: Enter today's date in the 'Start Date' field (leave the reason unselected and declaration unchecked)
Step Expected Result: Today's date is entered in the 'Start Date' field | The 'Save Draft' button remains disabled | The 'Submit Application' button remains disabled

TestCase Ticket Summary: Start Date and Reason Only — Application Cannot be Submitted
Ticket Description: When start date and reason are entered but the declaration is not checked, the application should remain blocked from saving or submitting.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership application form
Step Expected Result: The 'Save Draft' button is disabled | The 'Submit Application' button is disabled

Step Action: Enter today's date in the 'Start Date' field
Step Expected Result: Today's date is entered in the 'Start Date' field

Step Action: Select any reason from the 'Reason for Non-Practising Status' dropdown
Step Expected Result: A reason is selected | The 'Save Draft' button remains disabled | The 'Submit Application' button remains disabled

TestCase Ticket Summary: All Required Fields Complete — Application Ready for Submission
Ticket Description: When the start date, reason, and declaration are all completed, both the Save Draft and Submit Application buttons should become available.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership application form
Step Expected Result: The 'Save Draft' button is disabled | The 'Submit Application' button is disabled

Step Action: Enter today's date in the 'Start Date' field
Step Expected Result: Today's date is entered in the 'Start Date' field

Step Action: Select any reason from the 'Reason for Non-Practising Status' dropdown
Step Expected Result: A reason is selected

Step Action: Check the declaration checkbox
Step Expected Result: The declaration is now checked | The 'Save Draft' button becomes enabled | The 'Submit Application' button becomes enabled

Ticket Description: Teachers should be able to save their Non-Practising Membership application as a draft and retrieve it later from My Applications.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership application form
Step Expected Result: The form is displayed | The 'Save Draft' button is disabled

Step Action: Enter today's date in the 'Start Date' field
Step Expected Result: Today's date is entered in the 'Start Date' field

Step Action: Select any reason from the 'Reason for Non-Practising Status' dropdown
Step Expected Result: A reason is selected

Step Action: Check the declaration checkbox
Step Expected Result: The declaration is now checked | The 'Save Draft' button becomes enabled

Step Action: Click the 'Save Draft' button
Step Expected Result: The application is saved | The teacher is redirected to My Applications or sees a confirmation message | The Non-Practising Membership application appears in My Applications with a 'Draft' status

Ticket Description: Teachers should be able to submit their Non-Practising Membership application when all required fields are completed. The application should be automatically approved.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership application form
Step Expected Result: The form is displayed | The 'Submit Application' button is disabled

Step Action: Enter today's date in the 'Start Date' field
Step Expected Result: Today's date is entered in the 'Start Date' field

Step Action: Select any reason from the 'Reason for Non-Practising Status' dropdown
Step Expected Result: A reason is selected

Step Action: Check the declaration checkbox
Step Expected Result: The declaration is now checked | The 'Submit Application' button becomes enabled

Step Action: Click the 'Submit Application' button
Step Expected Result: A success message appears: 'Congratulations, your Non-practising teacher application has been submitted.' | A 'Back' button is visible on the confirmation page | The Non-Practising Membership application is automatically approved | My Applications shows the application with an 'Approved' status | A confirmation email is sent to the teacher

Ticket Description: If a teacher already has a draft or submitted Non-Practising Membership application, they should not be able to create another one.
Step Action: Navigate to My Applications > Create (teacher already has a draft or submitted Non-Practising Membership application)
Step Expected Result: The application selection page appears | The 'Non-practising teacher' option is visible

Step Action: Click the 'Non-practising teacher' option
Step Expected Result: An error message appears: 'You have already started an application for Non-practising teacher accreditation. Please update and submit the existing application to change your accreditation to Non-practising.'

Ticket Description: Teachers undergoing a Maintenance of Accreditation review should not be able to submit a Non-Practising Membership application until the review is complete.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership application form (teacher has accreditation review in progress)
Step Expected Result: The form is displayed | The 'Submit Application' button is disabled

Step Action: Enter today's date in the 'Start Date' field
Step Expected Result: Today's date is entered in the 'Start Date' field

Step Action: Select any reason from the 'Reason for Non-Practising Status' dropdown
Step Expected Result: A reason is selected

Step Action: Check the declaration checkbox
Step Expected Result: The declaration is now checked | The 'Submit Application' button becomes enabled

Step Action: Click the 'Submit Application' button
Step Expected Result: An error message appears: 'Maintenance of accreditation is in progress. You cannot apply for non-practising teacher accreditation at this time. Once a maintenance of accreditation decision has been made you can continue with your application.'

TestCase Ticket Summary: Block Submission if Accreditation Maintenance is Overdue
Ticket Description: Teachers with overdue Maintenance of Accreditation should not be able to submit a Non-Practising Membership application.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership application form (teacher has overdue accreditation maintenance)
Step Expected Result: The form is displayed | The 'Submit Application' button is disabled

Step Action: Enter today's date in the 'Start Date' field
Step Expected Result: Today's date is entered in the 'Start Date' field

Step Action: Select any reason from the 'Reason for Non-Practising Status' dropdown
Step Expected Result: A reason is selected

Step Action: Check the declaration checkbox
Step Expected Result: The declaration is now checked | The 'Submit Application' button becomes enabled

Step Action: Click the 'Submit Application' button
Step Expected Result: An error message appears: 'You are currently overdue to complete your accreditation maintenance. You can continue with your application after you submit your current maintenance. Accreditation maintenance decisions are made within 28 days. See eTAMS Help for instructions.'

TestCase Ticket Summary: Access Reactivation Form from My Applications
Ticket Description: Teachers with an approved Non-Practising Membership should be able to open the reactivation form from My Applications.
Step Action: Navigate to My Applications
Step Expected Result: The applications list is displayed | The approved Non-Practising Membership application is listed with 'Approved' status

Step Action: Click the approved Non-Practising Membership application
Step Expected Result: The 'Return to Active Status' section is displayed | Information about returning to active teaching is shown with a link to the NESA website | The 'End Date' field is present | The 'Update End Date' button is present | The 'Return to Active Status' button is present

TestCase Ticket Summary: Access Reactivation Form from Dashboard Tile
Ticket Description: Teachers with an approved Non-Practising Membership should be able to open the reactivation form from the Non-Practising teacher tile on the Dashboard.
Step Action: Navigate to the Dashboard
Step Expected Result: The Dashboard loads successfully | A Non-Practising teacher status tile is visible on the Dashboard

Step Action: Click the Non-Practising teacher tile
Step Expected Result: The 'Return to Active Status' section is displayed | Information about returning to active teaching is shown | The 'End Date' field is present | The 'Update End Date' button is present | The 'Return to Active Status' button is present

Ticket Description: The system should not allow reactivation with an end date that is on or before the start date of the Non-Practising Membership.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership reactivation form
Step Expected Result: The 'Update End Date' button is disabled | The 'Return to Active Status' button is disabled

Step Action: Enter an end date that is on or before the Non-Practising start date
Step Expected Result: The 'Update End Date' button remains disabled | The 'Return to Active Status' button remains disabled

Ticket Description: The system should allow reactivation when the end date is set to today's date.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership reactivation form
Step Expected Result: The 'Update End Date' button is disabled | The 'Return to Active Status' button is disabled

Step Action: Enter today's date as the end date
Step Expected Result: The 'Update End Date' button becomes enabled | The 'Return to Active Status' button becomes enabled

TestCase Ticket Summary: Block Reactivation if Working with Children Check Expired
Ticket Description: Teachers with an expired Working with Children Check should not be able to return to active teaching status.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership reactivation form (teacher has an expired Working with Children Check)
Step Expected Result: The 'Return to Active Status' section is visible

Step Action: Enter today's date as the end date and click the 'Return to Active Status' button
Step Expected Result: An error message appears: 'You will need to update your Working with Children Check Clearance before you can end your Non-practising teacher membership and reactivate your accreditation.'

TestCase Ticket Summary: Reactivate Within 5 Years — Retain Current Accreditation Level
Ticket Description: When a teacher returns to active status within 5 years of becoming Non-Practising, they should retain their previous accreditation level.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership reactivation form (teacher has been Non-Practising for less than 5 years and has a valid Working with Children Check)
Step Expected Result: The 'Return to Active Status' section is displayed | The Non-Practising start date is visible and shows a period of less than 5 years

Step Action: Enter today's date as the end date
Step Expected Result: The 'Return to Active Status' button becomes enabled

Step Action: Click the 'Return to Active Status' button
Step Expected Result: A success message appears: 'You have successfully returned to active accreditation.' | The Non-Practising Membership status changes to 'Completed' | The teacher's accreditation level remains as it was before becoming Non-Practising

TestCase Ticket Summary: Reactivate After 5 Years — Return to Proficient Teacher Level
Ticket Description: When a teacher returns to active status after more than 5 years of being Non-Practising, they return to Proficient Teacher level with a new 5-year maintenance period.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership reactivation form (teacher has been Non-Practising for more than 5 years and has a valid Working with Children Check)
Step Expected Result: The 'Return to Active Status' section is displayed | The Non-Practising start date is visible and shows a period of more than 5 years

Step Action: Enter an end date that results in a Non-Practising period of more than 5 years
Step Expected Result: The 'Return to Active Status' button becomes enabled

Step Action: Click the 'Return to Active Status' button
Step Expected Result: A success message appears: 'You have successfully returned to active accreditation.' | The Non-Practising Membership status changes to 'Completed' | The teacher's accreditation level is now Proficient Teacher | A new 5-year maintenance period is created

Ticket Description: The Non-Practising Membership application form should meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards.
Step Action: Open the Non-Practising Membership application form
Step Expected Result: The page meets all WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility requirements with no reported violations
